misc_info_2: [From ODNB] Began training under the foreman of his father's workshops about 1847, at age 15. Living with his father at 111 Minories at 1851 Census. Attended classes at Royal College of Chemistry under Prof.A.W.Hoffmann. Took over his father's business in 1856, trading as Spencer, Browning & Co.; Elected to Microscopical Soc. Of London 1863; Fellow Met.Soc. 1865-74; member RI 1871-82; Member RAS 1865 & on council 1870-73. Reconstructed self-registering meteorological instruments at Royal Observatory, Greenwich c.1870. Employed 61 workers, according to 1871 Census. Wrote numerous books & pamphlets on optical subjects. Employed Adam Hilger, who became his foreman.
misc_info_3: Founder member of British Optical Association & the first President 1895-1900.
misc_info_4: Browning retired about 1905 and went to live first at Kelsey, Glos. Then at Cheltenham, where he died at his home, Strathcona, Albion St, on 14 Dec 1925
